approval_notes: The owner-approved F004 plan authorises this as F004 Slice 1 (Safe fetch guard + adapter contract). The Chief implements it under the standing autonomy boundaries: may branch/implement/test/commit/push/open one PR; may NOT merge, flip any passes flag, add runtime dependencies, or advance to Slice 2.

F004 is the source-ingestion epic. Slice 1 establishes the sole network seam (a safe fetcher) and the SourceAdapter contract with typed carriers, so later slices (provider adapters, extraction, evidence, health, conflicts) build on a proven-safe foundation.
F004 stays passes:false after this slice. A fresh-context Level 2 slice evaluation reviews Slice 1 before Slice 2 proceeds; flipping F004 passes:true requires a full-epic Level 2 evaluation.
Stdlib-only: the live transport uses urllib/http.client from the Python standard library. No new runtime (or npm) dependency is added; the requirements-sync guard stays green.
objective: Establish the safe source-fetching seam and the source-adapter contract for the ingestion epic. Introduce a SafeFetcher whose network policy is expressed as pure, independently-testable functions (official-domain allowlist, SSRF/private-network blocking, https-only scheme allowlist, MIME validation, bounded redirects with per-hop re-validation, connect/read timeouts, and a streamed max-size cap with early abort) over a thin I/O layer. The default fetcher opens no socket; a live urllib-based transport is gated behind an explicit flag that is disabled by default. Define the SourceAdapter ABC enforcing the seven contract methods plus typed carriers, the closed verification-state vocabulary, a deterministic offline FixtureFetcher, and one minimal reference JSON adapter that makes the contract concrete.
scope: apps/api/app/ingest/fetch.py: pure policy functions (scheme allowlist, host/official-domain allowlist with subdomain match, IP address block classification for loopback/RFC1918/link-local 169.254/16/ULA fc00::7/metadata 169.254.169.254/unspecified/reserved/mapped, MIME parse+validate, redirect-budget check, size-cap check); a Fetcher Protocol; typed FetchResult (content, mime, final_url, content_hash sha256, fetched_at, status); a FetchPolicy config dataclass; a typed FetchError hierarchy; a default OfflineFetcher that never opens a socket; a live urllib-based LiveFetcher gated behind an explicit enable_network flag (disabled by default) that re-runs the host allowlist and SSRF checks on every redirect hop, streams with an early size abort, and enforces timeouts; and a deterministic offline FixtureFetcher test transport.

apps/api/app/ingest/base.py: the SourceAdapter abc.ABC enforcing discover/fetch/canonicalize/extract/validate/evidence/health, plus frozen typed carriers SourceDocument, CandidateFacts, EvidenceLocation, AdapterHealth. Adapters depend only on the Fetcher protocol (the sole network seam) and never import an HTTP client directly.

apps/api/app/ingest/vocab.py: the closed VERIFICATION_STATES vocabulary (detected, extracting, candidate, verified, verified_with_caveats, conflict, stale, withdrawn, rejected) mirroring docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, plus a small membership helper.

apps/api/app/ingest/reference.py: a minimal reference structured/JSON SourceAdapter that discovers configured URLs, fetches via the injected Fetcher, canonicalizes JSON, extracts CandidateFacts, validates required fields, emits EvidenceLocation, and reports AdapterHealth. Produces candidate facts only (never verified).
tests/unit/test_ingest_fetch.py and tests/unit/test_ingest_contract.py (offline; no external network egress).
docs: a short 'Safe source fetching + adapter contract' note (docs/DATA_MODEL.md or docs/PROVIDER_ADAPTERS.md) documenting the network policy, the network seam, and the contract.
out_of_scope: Any Alembic migration or database write (F004 Slice 1 adds no schema and writes no rows).
Real provider adapters, MCP/RSS/HTML/browser transports, or live provider scanning.
Persisting candidates/evidence/snapshots, ScanRun orchestration, change detection, conflict review, or offer_version mutation (offer_version immutability is untouched).
API routes, worker/scheduler wiring, or any publication path; no user-controlled URLs in public endpoints; no direct LLM-to-publication path.
Marking F004 (or any feature) passes:true; adding managed-cloud services or heavyweight dependencies; advancing to Slice 2.
acceptance_criteria: A non-allowlisted host is rejected PRE-connect (the host allowlist check fires before DNS resolution or any socket use).
A redirect to a disallowed or private host is rejected MID-chain (the host allowlist and SSRF address checks re-run on every redirect hop).
A non-https URL is rejected by the scheme allowlist (default allowed schemes = {https}).

Private, loopback, link-local, and cloud-metadata IPs (127.0.0.1, ::1, RFC1918 10/172.16/192.168, 169.254.0.0/16 incl. 169.254.169.254, fe80::/10, fc00::/7, 0.0.0.0, IPv4-mapped) are rejected by the SSRF address classifier.
A slow response aborts within the timeout budget (raises a typed timeout error).
An oversized response is aborted early by the streamed size cap (raises a typed too-large error) without buffering the whole body.
A disallowed MIME type is rejected by MIME validation.
The default fetcher (OfflineFetcher) opens NO socket, and the live transport is disabled by default (constructing/using it without the explicit enable_network flag fails).
The SourceAdapter ABC cannot be instantiated when any of the seven contract methods (discover, fetch, canonicalize, extract, validate, evidence, health) is missing.

The reference JSON adapter runs end-to-end offline through the FixtureFetcher and produces CandidateFacts (verification_state=candidate, never verified) with EvidenceLocation and an AdapterHealth report.
The verification-state vocabulary is closed and exactly matches docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.

Regression green: scripts/test.ps1 and scripts/check.ps1 -NodeAudit both pass offline (ruff, format, pytest, prettier, eslint, detect-secrets, pip-audit, npm audit), including the requirements-sync guard (no new dependency); no external network egress occurs in the suite or CI (the single loopback test binds 127.0.0.1 and allowlists it for that test only).
No unsupported free-tier/Z0 product claim is introduced; no DB migration or row write is added; offer_version immutability is not touched.
A fresh-context independent Level 2 evaluator records an adversarial verdict for Slice 1 in agent-state/evaluation.json before any status change is claimed; F004 remains passes:false.

end_to_end_steps: Run tests/unit/test_ingest_fetch.py and tests/unit/test_ingest_contract.py and confirm every acceptance criterion is exercised offline.
Run scripts/test.ps1 and scripts/check.ps1 -NodeAudit and confirm both pass end-to-end (no new dependency; requirements-sync green).
Confirm detect-secrets is green and no credential appears in code or output.
Confirm the live stack cycle is unaffected (the seam adds no runtime coupling).
negative_tests: fetch to a non-allowlisted host => DisallowedHostError before any socket/getaddrinfo call.
302 redirect to a non-allowlisted host => DisallowedHostError mid-chain.
302 redirect to an allowlisted host that is an RFC1918 IP => BlockedAddressError mid-chain (loopback allowance does not cover private ranges).
http:// URL with https-only policy => DisallowedSchemeError.
loopback server that sleeps beyond read_timeout => FetchTimeoutError within budget.
loopback response larger than max_bytes => ResponseTooLargeError, body not fully buffered.
response Content-Type outside the allowed MIME set => DisallowedMimeError.
OfflineFetcher.fetch with socket patched to fail => raises NetworkDisabledError without touching the socket.
SourceAdapter subclass missing any contract method => TypeError on instantiation.

risks.data_migration: None. This slice adds no schema and no migration; it introduces a network seam and a contract only.
risks.security_privacy: The whole point of the slice is defence in depth against SSRF and egress abuse. Policy is pure and unit-tested; the live transport is off by default; no credentials are handled; detect-secrets stays green. The one loopback test binds 127.0.0.1 and allowlists it only for that test, with no external egress.
risks.portability: Pure standard-library Python (urllib/http.client/ipaddress/socket); no managed-cloud dependency; runs identically on amd64 and arm64.
risks.zero_cost_safety: No offer data or free/Z0 claim is added; adapters produce candidate facts only and never publish.
risks.regression: No dependency change, so the requirements-sync guard is unaffected. The package is additive and imported by nothing at runtime yet, so existing services and tests are unchanged.
risks.supply_chain: No new dependency (Python or npm) is added by this slice.

notes: Owner approved the F004 plan and directed the builder to implement Slice 1 only, then stop for a fresh-context Level 2 slice evaluation before Slice 2. This slice does not mark F004 passing.
